description: baseline
scratch buffer reuse in count_into_bins (avoid 100K per-row Vec allocs)
O(1) uniform bin assignment replaces O(log n) binary search for bins_int/range modes
skip edges Vec alloc for bins_int (100K heap allocs eliminated)
ListPrimitiveChunkedBuilder flat buffer: eliminates 100K Series allocs, single pre-allocated output buffer
UInt16 output dtype: polars 0.51 ListPrimitiveChunkedBuilder<UInt16Type> panics; Python cast adds 20+10MB peak
cache finite values in Vec: 1 Polars pass + 2 Vec passes vs 2 Polars passes in bins_int
direct Arrow slice access in bins_int fast path (get_as_series not bottleneck; scatter-add is)
4-buffer scatter-add in bins_int: 4 independent accumulator arrays break load-store dependency chain, 13% wall time reduction
unsafe get_unchecked_mut in scatter-add (no gain: LLVM already elides bounds checks in release)
parallel std::thread::scope for bins_int: 51% wall time improvement but flat buffer + builder coexist (2x memory peak); fitness 1.552 > best 1.541
cont_slice() fast path for null-free rows: avoids Option<f64> per-element overhead in ChunkedArray iter, 32% wall time reduction
skip values_cache copy for all-finite rows (combined finiteness+min/max in 1 pass, count from slice): no significant gain — bandwidth saving offset by extra slice scans
8-buffer scatter-add: 8 independent accumulators regressed 45% vs 4-buffer (larger working set degrades cache/ILP balance)
parallel bins_int flat-buffer: 16 threads via std::thread::scope with single pre-allocated output Vec<u32> (no per-thread builders → no memory duplication); 55% wall time reduction, memory unchanged
direct Arrow buffer access in parallel bins_int: bypass get_as_series/cast/cont_slice per row + combined filter+min/max pass; no gain — per-row API overhead negligible vs scatter-add computation
UInt16 output via direct Arrow PrimitiveArray: polars 0.51 not-implemented panic in LargeList<UInt16> expression evaluation path
fuse filter+copy+min/max into single loop in parallel bins_int fast path: eliminates second scan of values_cache, -0.4% fitness
two-pass direct scatter-add: skip values_cache copy for all-finite rows; min/max extra pass offsets bandwidth savings
target-cpu=native: recompile all 22 crates with AVX-512 and native instruction set — Polars internals + scatter-add benefit from wider SIMD; 14% wall time reduction
codegen-units=1 + lto=thin: wall_time regressed 5% — LTO likely interferes with LLVM's scatter-add vectorization or inlining decisions
physical-cores-only (8 threads vs 16): +95% wall time regression — HT pairs help hide latency in scatter-add, 16 threads needed for full throughput
baseline re-run on new host (mold removed, target-cpu=native retained)
lto=thin with codegen-units=4: 7.7% wall time reduction via cross-module inlining; memory within noise
lto=fat slightly slower than lto=thin (200.1ms vs 199.5ms) — thin LTO wins
8-value 4-buffer scatter-add: 5% wall time regression vs 4-value — same-bin conflicts or ILP overhead
split filter+copy+min/max into 2 passes: 60% wall time regression — fused loop wins
direct Arrow buffer access: skip per-row get_as_series+cast+f64 — 15% wall time reduction
two-pass: eliminate values_cache — scatter-add directly on original slice (2% wall time reduction)
two-loop scatter: vectorizable index computation (loop1) + unsafe scatter-add (loop2) — 25% wall time reduction
